title: Profiling, Event-Loop Health, Memory, Load, and Capacity · Contract Lens: Capacity Littles Law
summary: A team optimizes microbenchmarks, measures only average latency, ignores coordinated omission and warmup, raises concurrency until throughput peaks, and has no resource or cost model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts capacity littles law increment in performance experiment and capacity envelope. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate arrival rate, concurrency, service time, queueing, utilization, memory, pool limits, instance count, cost, and headroom without treating estimates as guarantees.
Model arrival rate, concurrency, payload, endpoint mix, warmup, caches, connection reuse, percentiles, coordinated omission, errors, and saturation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Capacity Littles Law · Theory Capacity Littles Law
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 5504 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate tail latency with event-loop and utilization capacity evidence; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then make OpenAPI and runtime disagree on nullability and block the release. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Capacity Littles Law contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Predict Capacity Littles Law · Theory Capacity Littles Law.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate arrival rate, concurrency, service time, queueing, utilization, memory, pool limits, instance count, cost, and headroom without treating estimates as guarantees.
